description: Vibestrate - local-first autonomous multi-agent completion orchestrator. Runs your local agent CLIs through plan → architect → implement → validate → review → fix → verify in isolated git worktrees.

commands.name: init
commands.description: Initialize Vibestrate in the current project (.vibestrate/ scaffold).

options.description: re-scaffold / overwrite existing config files to repair a broken project (runs are preserved)
non-interactive: use safe detected defaults, never wait for input
force the flowd wizard even when --yes would default to non-interactive
if the directory isn't a git repo, create one (starter .gitignore; initial commit only when no secret-like files would be swept). Never implied by --yes.

commands.name: setup
commands.description: Flowd wizard for provider, validation commands, and run defaults.

commands.name: provider
commands.description: Inspect, configure, and test local coding-CLI providers.

subcommands.name: detect
subcommands.description: Scan PATH for known local coding CLIs (claude/codex/opencode/aider/ollama).

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Show providers configured in this project.

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: test
subcommands.description: Send a tiny no-op prompt to a configured provider and look for the magic token.

options.description: skip confirmation prompt (non-interactive)

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Assign every default agent to use the given provider.

options.description: skip confirmation prompts when adding a detected provider

subcommands.name: setup
subcommands.description: Flowd provider setup wizard.

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Remove a provider from project.yml (refuses if a role still uses it).

options.description: skip the confirmation prompt (non-interactive)

subcommands.name: catalog
subcommands.description: Show the provider capability catalog (built-in + your .vibestrate/providers-catalog.yml overlay).

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: refresh
subcommands.description: Detect each provider's real models/efforts (codex `debug models` JSON, else --help scraping) and write them to the catalog overlay. Refreshes stale built-in lists; local only.

options.description: replace existing overlay/built-in entries instead of gap-filling
show what would be written without writing

commands.name: config
commands.description: Show and edit .vibestrate/project.yml without hand-editing YAML.

subcommands.name: view
subcommands.description: Readable, grouped view of the config - each section shows where it's editable.

options.description: emit the structured view as JSON

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Print the raw config YAML and validate it.

options.description: emit parsed JSON instead of YAML

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: Print a single config value (dot-path, e.g. commands.validate).

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Set a config value. Booleans/numbers/strings parsed automatically; arrays/objects via JSON (e.g. 'vibe config set commands.validate "[\"pnpm test\"]"').

subcommands.name: keys
subcommands.description: List every settable config key with its type, allowed values, and default (from the schema).

subcommands.name: validate
subcommands.description: Validate the project.yml file against the Vibestrate schema.

options.description: emit JSON

commands.name: skills
commands.description: List, inspect, and assign skills (.vibestrate/skills and .claude/skills).

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Show every discovered skill and which agents use it.

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Print a skill's full SKILL.md body.

subcommands.name: assign
subcommands.description: Attach a skill to an agent (writes to .vibestrate/project.yml).

subcommands.name: unassign
subcommands.description: Remove a skill from an agent.

subcommands.name: fetch
subcommands.description: Fetch a skill markdown from an http(s) URL into .vibestrate/skills/ (guarded + secret-redacted).

options.description: skill filename (default: derived from the URL)
read-only AI overview: helpful / already-present / conflicting
replace an existing skill of the same name

commands.name: flows
commands.description: List and inspect Flow run recipes from built-ins and .vibestrate/flows.

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Show every discovered Flow.

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Print a Flow's seats, ordered steps, and crew seat-coverage.

options.description: emit JSON
check coverage against this crew (default: project default)

subcommands.name: use
subcommands.description: Set the default Flow applied to runs without --flow (always shown), or --clear it.

options.description: clear the default flow so the orchestrator selects per task

subcommands.name: suggest
subcommands.description: Suggest a Flow from task risk signals and local Flow outcomes.

options.description: known touched file path; repeat for more
task risk level: low, medium, or high
emit JSON

subcommands.name: export
subcommands.description: Export a Flow as canonical YAML (for sharing / backup).

options.description: write the YAML to a file instead of stdout
emit JSON { flowId, source, yaml }

subcommands.name: import
subcommands.description: Import a Flow from a local file path or an http(s) URL into .vibestrate/flows/.

options.description: replace an existing project flow with the same id
emit JSON

subcommands.name: export-arbitration
subcommands.description: Export a Quality Arbitration run as local JSON evidence for later evaluation.

options.description: write the JSON export to a local file

subcommands.name: hub
subcommands.description: Browse + install Flows from the community hub (vibestrate.com/api/hub).

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List (or search) Flows in the hub.

options.description: override the hub base URL
emit JSON

subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: Pull + verify + install a hub Flow (by ref) into .vibestrate/flows/.

options.description: override the hub base URL
replace an existing project flow with the same id

subcommands.name: publish
subcommands.description: Publish a project flow to the hub (public, immutable).

options.description: the release version, e.g. 1.2.0
hub name (defaults to the flow id)
your GitHub login (must match the token account)
override the hub base URL
send the token to a non-default origin (local testing)
skip the confirmation prompt
emit JSON

commands.name: supervisor
commands.description: Supervisor personas (the orchestrator's judgment posture). See orchestrator-personas.md.

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List the resolved supervisor personas (built-ins + project).

options.description: emit JSON

commands.name: profile
commands.description: Runtime presets (provider + model/power) that Crew roles run on.

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List profiles, grouped by provider, with how many roles use each.

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Create a new profile.

options.description: raw provider id this profile runs on
human label
provider model id (e.g. sonnet, opus)
provider-specific power/effort (e.g. balanced)
hard cap on output tokens per turn
per-turn wall-clock timeout (ms)

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Edit an existing profile's fields.

options.description: human label
provider model id (e.g. sonnet, opus)
provider-specific power/effort (e.g. balanced)
hard cap on output tokens per turn
per-turn wall-clock timeout (ms)
move the profile to a different provider

subcommands.name: duplicate
subcommands.description: Copy a profile under a new id (e.g. claude -> claude-cheap).

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Delete a profile (refuses if a role uses it, unless --force).

options.description: delete even if roles still reference it

commands.name: params
commands.description: Durable project parameters: typed param answers persisted + reused across runs.

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List every stored param answer (secrets shown as env refs).

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: Print one stored value (secrets shown as env refs).

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Set one or more values: `vibe params set --flow <id> name=Acme niche=SaaS`. With --flow, keys are flow params (type-checked, secret-aware). Without it, keys are raw param keys (bare = project-global).

options.description: interpret keys as the flow's declared params (recommended)

subcommands.name: generate
subcommands.description: Draft a value for a `generate`-enabled param via a provider (optional, user-initiated, reviewed). Prints a suggestion; --accept stores it.

options.description: the flow that declares the param
store the suggestion (setBy: generated) without asking

subcommands.name: unset
subcommands.description: Remove stored values by key (explicit, never automatic).

commands.name: crew
commands.description: List crews, show a crew's roles, and set the default ("active") crew.

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List configured crews (the default is marked).

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Show a crew's roles, profiles, and seats (default crew if omitted).

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: use
subcommands.description: Set the default ("active") crew - runs without --crew use it.

subcommands.name: presets
subcommands.description: Ready-made crews (fast / thorough) tuned by provider effort.

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List available presets and whether they're installed.

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Install a preset crew (fast / thorough) into project.yml.

commands.name: approvals
commands.description: Inspect and resolve human-approval requests for a paused run.

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Show all approval requests for a run.

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Show a single approval request in detail.

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: approve
subcommands.description: Approve a pending approval. Resumes the run if it is waiting.

options.description: decision note recorded in approvals.json

subcommands.name: reject
subcommands.description: Reject a pending approval. The run will be marked `blocked`.

options.description: decision note recorded in approvals.json

commands.name: roadmap
commands.description: Manage local roadmap items (.vibestrate/roadmap/roadmap.json).

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Create the .vibestrate/roadmap/ scaffold if missing.

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Add a roadmap item.

options.description: longer description
low | medium | high
emit JSON

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List roadmap items.

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Show a single roadmap item.

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Update a roadmap item.

subcommands.name: archive
subcommands.description: Archive a roadmap item (keeps history).

subcommands.name: proposals
subcommands.description: List roadmap proposals stored in .vibestrate/roadmap/proposals/.

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: proposal
subcommands.description: Inspect, parse, and accept individual proposals.

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Print a proposal's raw Markdown body.

subcommands.name: parse
subcommands.description: Parse a proposal and print the typed preview.

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: accept
subcommands.description: Accept a parsed proposal (creates roadmap items + tasks atomically).

options.description: preview without writing
skip DEPENDS_ON entries that point at unknown task titles instead of failing
emit JSON

subcommands.name: plan
subcommands.description: Run the configured local planner provider on a broad goal and save the output as a proposal draft.

options.description: explicit proposal id; default is timestamp + slug
override the provider id (default: planner agent's provider)

commands.name: spec-up
commands.description: Plan as a CTO: discovery -> spec -> architecture -> roadmap (a chain of read-only runs).

subcommands.name: start
subcommands.description: Start spec-up: launch the intake run that asks the gap questions.

options.description: supervisor persona (judgment posture) for the run
the flow to BUILD once the spec is approved (carried to `spec-up build`)

subcommands.name: questions
subcommands.description: Show the intake run's gap questions (and their ids).

options.description: emit the questions as JSON

subcommands.name: answer
subcommands.description: Answer a round's questions; loops to a gap-check round or builds the spec.

options.description: answer for a question id (repeatable)
stop questioning and build the spec now (skip further gap-checks)

subcommands.name: simplify
subcommands.description: Explain a question in plain language (what it asks + what it affects).

options.description: add an everyday-life analogy (no jargon)

subcommands.name: suggest
subcommands.description: Draft an answer grounded in your prior answers (you still decide). --all for every blank.

options.description: suggest a draft for every question in the round

subcommands.name: approve
subcommands.description: Approve the spec-up draft and launch the roadmap synthesis run.

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: Approve the spec-up draft and BUILD it: run the chosen flow seeded with the approved spec.

options.description: build flow override (default: the flow carried from the spec-up run)

subcommands.name: roadmap
subcommands.description: Turn a finished spec-up-roadmap run into a reviewable proposal.

subcommands.name: edit
subcommands.description: Edit a spec-up section (scope/spec/architecture/risks) before the build, via $EDITOR or --file. Guarded: secret-refusing, blocked after approve.

options.description: read the new content from a file instead of opening $EDITOR

commands.name: tasks
commands.description: Manage local tasks: backlog → queued → running → done.

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Create a task.

options.description: low | medium | high
link to a roadmap item id
comma-separated skill names
comma-separated likely-touched files
override the provider for runs spawned from this task.
investigation-only: runs spawned from this task skip executor + fix loop and refuse apply/validate/revert.
emit JSON

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List tasks.

options.description: filter by status
emit JSON

subcommands.name: suggest
subcommands.description: Suggest which backlog card to pick up next (ready + priority).

options.description: show the full ranked backlog, not just the top few
emit JSON

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Show a task with comments and run history.

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: comment
subcommands.description: Add a comment to a task.

subcommands.name: ready
subcommands.description: Mark a task ready to run.

subcommands.name: cancel
subcommands.description: Cancel a task.

subcommands.name: archive
subcommands.description: Archive a task (files it into the board's Archived column).

subcommands.name: unarchive
subcommands.description: Un-archive a task.

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Permanently remove a task card (refuses while its run is live).

options.description: skip the confirmation prompt

subcommands.name: queue
subcommands.description: Add a task to the scheduler queue.

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: Run this task now (foreground; same as vibe run --task <id>).

subcommands.name: pickup
subcommands.description: Execute the task's checklist item-by-item (pick-up flow). Continuous by default; --step pauses between items.

options.description: pause between items for review (step-by-step)

subcommands.name: checklist
subcommands.description: Manage a task's in-card checklist (the ordered breakdown of items).

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List a task's checklist items.

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Append a checklist item.

subcommands.name: check
subcommands.description: Mark a checklist item done.

subcommands.name: uncheck
subcommands.description: Reset a checklist item to pending.

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Set an item status: pending | in_progress | done | blocked.

subcommands.name: edit
subcommands.description: Edit a checklist item's text.

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Remove a checklist item.

subcommands.name: move
subcommands.description: Move a checklist item to a 1-based position.

subcommands.name: promote
subcommands.description: Promote a checklist item to its own card (keeps a derived-from link).

subcommands.name: enhance
subcommands.description: Propose a checklist for a task with an AI assist (read-only). Add --apply to append the items.

options.description: append the proposed items to the task's checklist
profile to run the assist on (default: crew planner)
emit JSON

subcommands.name: report
subcommands.description: Generate a Markdown task report at .vibestrate/roadmap/tasks/<id>-report.md.

commands.name: integrate
commands.description: Preview + integrate merge-ready run branches into a dedicated branch (never main, never push).

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List merge-ready runs (integration candidates).

subcommands.name: preview
subcommands.description: Dry-run merge the selected (or all) merge-ready branches; show conflicts.

subcommands.name: advise
subcommands.description: Read-only merge advice for the selected (or all) merge-ready runs: risk flags, assurance lanes, topology, dry-run conflicts, and a deterministic recommendation. Mutates nothing.

options.description: emit the advice as JSON

subcommands.name: analyze
subcommands.description: Optional read-only LLM pass over the run's redacted diff vs main: semantic risk narrative (never a merge verdict). Spawns a local provider; caches markdown under the run.

options.description: emit the full analysis result as JSON

subcommands.name: apply
subcommands.description: Integrate the selected (or all) merge-ready branches into --into <branch>.

options.description: the integration branch to create (never main)

subcommands.name: finish
subcommands.description: Merge a complete, clean integration branch into main - locally, with explicit confirmation, never pushed. Refuses partial integrations, dirty trees, and conflicts.

options.description: non-interactive consent: must be exactly "merge-to-main"

commands.name: queue
commands.description: Manage the local task scheduler queue.

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Show the queue and running tasks.

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Add a task to the queue.

options.description: origin label for fairness / per-source quotas (default: user)

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Remove a task from the queue.

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: Start the local scheduler loop and process queued tasks.

options.description: exit once the queue is empty (useful in scripts)

subcommands.name: pause
subcommands.description: Pause the scheduler (new tasks will not start).

subcommands.name: resume
subcommands.description: Resume the scheduler.

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Print scheduler state and recent conflict warnings.

options.description: emit JSON

commands.name: logs
commands.description: Show the captured provider stdout/stderr stream for a run (the model's live CLI output).

options.description: tail the stream live (like `tail -f`); Ctrl+C to stop
specific agent stream to read (default: newest)

commands.name: telemetry
commands.description: Opt-in OpenTelemetry export of a run's metrics to your own collector (off by default).

subcommands.name: trace
subcommands.description: Print the OTLP trace JSON for a run (no network - inspect before exporting).

subcommands.name: export
subcommands.description: Export a run's metrics as an OTLP trace to a collector (Langfuse, Tempo, Jaeger…).

options.description: OTLP/HTTP base URL (e.g. http://localhost:4318)
env reference for a bearer token, e.g. env:LANGFUSE_TOKEN

commands.name: workspace
commands.description: Track + switch between multiple Vibestrate projects (a user-level registry).

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List registered projects (live ● / dormant ○).

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Register a project directory (default: the current directory).

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Remove a project from the workspace registry (leaves it on disk).

subcommands.name: open
subcommands.description: Open a project's dashboard, starting it (server + scheduler) if dormant.

options.description: open every registered project
start the dashboard(s) but don't open a browser tab

subcommands.name: close
subcommands.description: Shut down a project's dashboard + scheduler (refuses if busy unless --force).

options.description: close every live registered project
shut down even if runs/queue are busy

subcommands.name: overview
subcommands.description: Cross-project rollup: runs + cost across every registered project.

options.description: window: 24h|7d|30d|90d
emit JSON

commands.name: notifications
commands.description: Inspect and manage local Vibestrate notifications.

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Show notifications.

options.description: only show unread
only show items that need action
emit JSON

subcommands.name: read
subcommands.description: Mark a notification read.

subcommands.name: resolve
subcommands.description: Mark a notification resolved.

subcommands.name: read-all
subcommands.description: Mark every unread notification as read.

subcommands.name: settings
subcommands.description: Show current notification settings and configured gateways.

subcommands.name: test
subcommands.description: Send a tiny test notification through a configured gateway.

commands.name: gateways
commands.description: Inspect and toggle notification delivery gateways.

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Show available gateways and their enabled/valid status.

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: test
subcommands.description: Send a test message through a gateway (no real notification persisted).

subcommands.name: enable
subcommands.description: Enable a configured gateway.

subcommands.name: disable
subcommands.description: Disable a gateway. Existing config is preserved.

commands.name: editor
commands.description: Configure and test the local editor handoff used by the dashboard.

subcommands.name: detect
subcommands.description: Probe known editors (code, code-insiders, cursor) for availability.

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Enable editor handoff and store the command (default args use --goto path:line:column).

options.description: override the default args (use {file}/{line}/{column} placeholders)

subcommands.name: test
subcommands.description: Open a file (default: README.md) using the configured editor command.

options.description: line number

commands.name: suggestions
commands.description: Inspect and act on review suggestions captured for a run.

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List every suggestion attached to a run.

options.description: emit JSON instead of a human-readable table

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Show one suggestion in detail (including any proposed patch).

subcommands.name: approve
subcommands.description: Approve a suggestion (creates and resolves an approval record).

options.description: decision note recorded with the approval

subcommands.name: reject
subcommands.description: Reject a suggestion. Records a rejection in approvals.json.

options.description: decision note recorded with the approval

subcommands.name: apply
subcommands.description: Apply an approved suggestion's proposedPatch inside the run's worktree (git apply, never push/merge).

options.description: after applying, run commands.validate inside the worktree
if validation fails, revert the patch (only valid with --validate)
validation profile to run after apply (only meaningful with --validate)

subcommands.name: validate
subcommands.description: Run the project's commands.validate inside the run's worktree against an applied suggestion.

options.description: named validation profile from commands.validationProfiles

subcommands.name: revert
subcommands.description: Revert a previously-applied suggestion using the captured patch (git apply -R).

subcommands.name: profile
subcommands.description: Read or edit a suggestion's validation profile metadata.

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Print the suggestion's current validation profile (if any).

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Set the suggestion's validation profile. Future validation runs use this profile. Does NOT re-run validation.

subcommands.name: clear
subcommands.description: Clear the suggestion's validation profile back to default (commands.validate).

commands.name: bundles
commands.description: Group reviewed suggestions into a review pass that applies, validates, and reverts as a unit.

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List every bundle (review pass) attached to a run.

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: create
subcommands.description: Create a new review pass (bundle) for a run.

options.description: human-readable title
optional longer description
initial suggestion ids to include (must belong to this run)

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Add a suggestion to a draft bundle.

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Remove a suggestion from a draft bundle.

subcommands.name: approve
subcommands.description: Approve a review pass (gate before apply).

options.description: decision note

subcommands.name: reject
subcommands.description: Reject a review pass.

options.description: decision note

subcommands.name: apply
subcommands.description: Apply every suggestion in the review pass to the run worktree (all-or-nothing with rollback).

options.description: after applying, run commands.validate
if validation fails, revert the bundle (only valid with --validate)
validation profile to run after apply (only meaningful with --validate)

subcommands.name: smart-apply
subcommands.description: Apply suggestions one-by-one in order. Earlier successes stay applied if a later step fails.

options.description: validate after each step; stop at the first failing validation
when --stop-on-validation-fail is set, revert ONLY the failing step (prior steps stay applied)
force every step to use this named validation profile
let each step use its own VALIDATION_PROFILE (falls back to bundle/default)

subcommands.name: validate
subcommands.description: Run commands.validate against the run worktree, attached to a bundle.

options.description: named validation profile from commands.validationProfiles

subcommands.name: revert
subcommands.description: Revert every suggestion in the review pass via git apply -R (worktree only).

subcommands.name: preflight
subcommands.description: Run a static-only preflight without modifying the worktree.

subcommands.name: profile
subcommands.description: Read or edit a review pass's validation profile metadata.

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Print the bundle's current validation profile (if any).

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Set the bundle's validation profile. Future validation runs use this profile. Does NOT re-run validation.

subcommands.name: clear
subcommands.description: Clear the bundle's validation profile back to default (commands.validate).

commands.name: validation
commands.description: Inspect validation profiles configured under commands.validationProfiles.

subcommands.name: profiles
subcommands.description: List the implicit default + every named validation profile.

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: usage
subcommands.description: Show how often each validation profile has actually run (`commands.validate` use counts as 'default').

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: profile
subcommands.description: Inspect, manage, and migrate validation profile references.

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Show the resolved commands for a named profile (or 'default').

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: migrate
subcommands.description: Rewrite suggestion and bundle records that reference <fromProfile> to point at <toProfile>. Use --clear to migrate to the default profile.

options.description: preview affected records; write nothing
interpret <toProfile> as a placeholder for clear-to-default
scan every run instead of the recent 50
limit to a single run

subcommands.name: clear-references
subcommands.description: Clear every suggestion/bundle that references <profileName> back to the default profile.

options.description: preview affected records; write nothing
scan every run instead of the recent 50
limit to a single run

subcommands.name: rename
subcommands.description: Rename a validation profile in project.yml AND migrate every suggestion/bundle reference in one atomic operation. Preserves the profile's description and commands. Refuses if <toProfile> already exists.

options.description: preview the project.yml rename + affected references; write nothing
scan every run instead of the recent 50
limit the reference scan to a single run

subcommands.name: doctor
subcommands.description: Audit every suggestion + bundle for stale validation-profile references. Default scope matches `vibe doctor` (recent 50 runs); use --all to lift the cap.

options.description: scan every run instead of the recent 50
limit the audit to a single run
emit JSON (machine-readable; includes didYouMean per record)

subcommands.name: migrations
subcommands.description: List previously-applied profile migrations.

options.description: emit JSON

commands.name: terminal
commands.description: Inspect and close dashboard terminal sessions. Sessions are user-launched from the dashboard; this CLI never spawns one.

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List every terminal session ever opened in this project (live + closed).

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: close
subcommands.description: Mark a terminal session as closed. Only affects live sessions in the running dashboard process; closed sessions are already terminal.

commands.name: policies
commands.description: Inspect user policy rules in .vibestrate/policies/. Rules can refuse a suggestion/bundle apply; they never permit a patch that built-in safety already refused.

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List every rule loaded from .vibestrate/policies/*.yml (after schema + regex/glob validation).

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: check
subcommands.description: Apply the loaded policy rules to a patch file (unified diff). Read-only - never applies, never executes.

options.description: which apply surface to simulate (suggestion-apply | bundle-apply)
emit JSON

subcommands.name: doctor
subcommands.description: Validate rule YAML, list malformed files, surface duplicate ids and empty-rule files.

options.description: emit JSON

subcommands.name: config
subcommands.description: Show or set the safety behavior toggles (strict apply-only, terminal, forbid-* guards).

options.description: emit JSON
agents propose diffs; gateway applies
read-only claude seats run --permission-mode plan
enable the dashboard terminal panel
block writes to the main branch
block reads/writes of secret files
block auto-push
block auto-merge

commands.name: assurance
commands.description: Show a run's Run Assurance verdict (evidence-backed; from the Action Broker log + review/verification).

arguments.description: the run id or display name (see `vibe status`)

options.description: emit JSON

commands.name: audit
commands.description: Show a run's audit tree (flow steps, per-step attempts incl. retries/fallbacks, control events).

arguments.description: the run id or display name (see `vibe status`)

options.description: emit JSON

commands.name: path
commands.description: Show a run's workspace (worktree path + branch) so you can cd into it.

options.description: print only the absolute worktree path (for `cd "$(...)"`)
emit JSON

commands.name: rename
commands.description: Give a run a friendly display name (the run id stays the same).

commands.name: ledger
commands.description: Show the project continuity brief (what shipped, what's open).

options.description: emit the structured ledger state as JSON
max entries per section (default 5)

commands.name: consult
commands.description: Ask the project orchestrator a question, answered from controlled project context (read-only).

arguments.description: the question to ask about this project

options.description: include a task's context (title, status, checklist)
focus a recent run by id
include a project file's content (repeatable)
answer with a specific profile (default: the crew's read-only planner)
answer ad-hoc with this provider (overrides --profile)
model for the ad-hoc provider (requires --provider)
effort/power for the ad-hoc provider (requires --provider)
emit the full structured result as JSON

commands.name: guide
commands.description: Manage VIBESTRATE.md (the agent's operating guide for this project) and its proposals.

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Print the project's VIBESTRATE.md (or note that there is none).

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Scaffold a starter VIBESTRATE.md at the project root (refuses if one exists).

subcommands.name: proposals
subcommands.description: List open VIBESTRATE.md proposals (e.g. from consult).

options.description: include applied/rejected proposals

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Show a proposal's full suggested text.

subcommands.name: apply
subcommands.description: Apply a proposal - append its text to VIBESTRATE.md (guarded write).

subcommands.name: reject
subcommands.description: Reject a proposal (keeps it on record, marked rejected).

commands.name: budget
commands.description: View or configure the daily spend cap (and what happens when it's hit).

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Show the configured cap, action, and today's spend so far.

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Set the daily spend cap and/or the action taken when it's reached.

options.description: daily cap in USD (e.g. 5)
what to do at the cap: stop | downgrade-model | reduce-effort
warn threshold as a fraction 0..1 (default 0.8)
cheaper Profile to switch to on downgrade-model
max agent turns in one run (count ceiling)
max wall-clock minutes for one run
max agent turns across all runs today
max wall-clock minutes across all runs today
what to do when a count/time ceiling is hit (pause = attended)

subcommands.name: off
subcommands.description: Remove the daily spend cap.

commands.name: replay
commands.description: Read-only inspector for a persisted run (mirrors the Replay tab in the dashboard).

arguments.description: id of the run to replay

options.description: emit the full replay projection as JSON

commands.name: pause
commands.description: Request that an active run pause at the next stage boundary.

arguments.description: id of the run to pause

commands.name: resume
commands.description: Clear a pending pause request or resume a paused run.

arguments.description: id of the run to resume

commands.name: shell
commands.description: Interactive terminal panel. For the full dashboard + scheduler + browser in one shot, use `vibe ui` instead.

options.description: snapshot refresh interval in ms (default 1000)
print one snapshot as JSON and exit (useful for scripts / smoke tests)

commands.name: run
commands.description: Run the default plan→architect→implement→review→verify workflow.

options.description: start the local supervisor dashboard alongside the run
port for the supervisor dashboard (default 4317)
link this run to a roadmap task; updates task status and runIds.
crew to resolve the flow's seats against (default: project.defaultCrew).
run-wide Profile override applied to every seated step in this run.
investigation-only run: skip executor + fix loop; refuse apply/validate/revert; force readOnly permissions on every role.
permission mode: read-only | ask (approve each change) | accept-edits (auto-apply, then hold for your sign-off before completing) | auto (default). --read-only is the alias for read-only.
never pause for a human: forces budget onLimit->stop and resilience onExhausted->fail, so the run always terminates on its own.
comma-separated skill ids to attach to every agent for this single run (merged with each agent's configured skills).
ask agents to produce token-efficient output (prefer diffs, bullets, no preamble).
resolve and run a Flow recipe for this run.
supervisor persona (judgment posture) for this run; default = project.defaultPersona.
let the orchestrator pick the Flow even when a default flow is set.
override the Profile for a Flow step (same Role, different runtime). Repeat for multiple steps.
pin a Role to a Seat when the crew has more than one role filling it. Repeat for multiple seats.
extra brief for the Flow task packet.
Flow context policy (balanced|compact|artifact-heavy).
skip an optional Flow step for this run. Repeat for multiple steps.
set a flow parameter (for a flow that declares `params:`). Repeatable.
interactively pick the Flow and Crew you didn't pass (horizontal selector), then run. With --flow, opens that flow's detailed setup instead.
rewind: fork from a prior run, reusing its plan (+ architecture) instead of regenerating them.
stage to resume at with --resume-from: planning | architecting | executing (regenerate code) | reviewing | fixing | verifying (restore the source run's code snapshot). Default: executing.
dry-run a downstream rewind: print the files the restore would overwrite/remove (vs the worktree base), then exit without starting a run. Use with --resume-from.
pick-up execution over the linked task's checklist: continuous | step. Needs --task and a checklist-aware flow (--flow pickup).
attach a project file as context for every agent (repeatable; path-guarded, secrets redacted).
attach an http(s) URL as context (repeatable; SSRF-guarded, bounded, secrets redacted).

commands.name: ui
commands.description: Start the local supervisor dashboard for this project.

options.description: port to bind (default 4317)
bind host (default 127.0.0.1). A non-loopback host exposes the API on the network and requires VIBESTRATE_API_TOKEN.
don't open the dashboard in your default browser on startup (default: open).
don't start the managed scheduler subprocess (default: on; the UI owns its lifecycle).

commands.name: status
commands.description: List Vibestrate runs in this project.

options.description: emit JSON instead of a human-readable table

commands.name: abort
commands.description: Mark a run as aborted (does not delete the worktree).

commands.name: runs
commands.description: Manage runs and their rewind snapshots.

subcommands.name: prune
subcommands.description: Delete rewind-snapshot refs you choose to drop: orphans (run dir gone), beyond a keep-N window, or one run. Shows the plan and confirms first; never purges on its own.

options.description: keep the N most-recent runs' snapshots, prune the rest
prune refs whose run directory is gone (default when no other scope is given)
prune one specific run's snapshots
show what would be pruned, delete nothing
skip the confirmation prompt

commands.name: doctor
commands.description: Check environment, config, providers, and recommend next steps.

options.description: emit JSON
apply safe fixes (create missing dirs/templates, add Claude provider if detected, suggest validation)
